title: Habit Stacking

content.statement: Attach new habits to existing ones.
content.question: What existing habit can you stack a new behavior onto tomorrow?
sop: List 3 habits you already do consistently without needing motivation (e.g. making coffee, brushing teeth).
Rate the Anchor strength 1-10 for each existing habit (see calculation, page 4)
Pick your strongest anchor and attach exactly one new specific behavior directly after it.
Rate the Attachment 1-10, how tightly and immediately the new habit follows the anchor (see calculation, page 4)
Repeat the pair daily for 2 weeks before adding a second stacked habit.
questions: What existing habit can you stack a new behavior onto tomorrow?
Which habit already runs on autopilot for you, strong enough to carry a second behavior along with it?
What new habit have you tried to start from scratch three times and failed, because it had no anchor?
content.equation_example: Rate Anchor strength 1-10 (how consistent the existing habit already is) and Attachment 1-10 (how tightly the new habit is linked right after it). Anchor 9 times Attachment 6 equals 54 out of 100. Higher means the new habit rides the old one's momentum reliably. Lower means the new habit is floating free and will get skipped.
content.closing_statement: New habits borrow their strength from old ones.

caption:
Principle Nº 137: Habit Stacking

Attach new habits to existing ones.

Ask yourself: What existing habit can you stack a new behavior onto tomorrow?
